title: Chapelieria melleri
intro: Chapelieria melleri, also known as Meller's Chapelieria, is a flowering plant species in the family Gentianaceae. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Chapelieria melleri include iridoid glycosides, such as gentiopicroside and loganic acid, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Chapelieria melleri include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to relieve respiratory congestion and promote relaxation.
